steps.title: Plot the table in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the table values as $x_1=[0,1,2,3]$ and $y_1=[0,1,8,27]$. Those points rise slowly at first and then very quickly. To test the likely cubic pattern, add $y_1\sim ax_1^3$; Desmos gives $a=1$, so the model is $y=x^3$.

steps.title: Confirm the pattern by hand
steps.content: Each row matches $y=x^3$: $0^3=0$, $1^3=1$, $2^3=8$, and $3^3=27$. The graph should pass through the origin, stay relatively flat near $x=0$, and then rise steeply as $x$ increases.

steps.title: Evaluate each graph choice
steps.content:
• Choice A shows a straight line or parabola — growth is too slow and the shape is wrong. Eliminated.
  • Choice B shows an S-shaped cubic curve passing through $(0,0)$, $(1,1)$, $(2,8)$, and $(3,27)$ with rapid upward growth — consistent with $y = x^3$. Kept.
  • Choice C shows a curve that is symmetric or behaves like an even-degree function — does not match $y = x^3$. Eliminated.
  • Choice D shows a curve with different concavity or a different intercept — inconsistent with the table values. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
